pattern: Each nonzero decimal digit contributes exactly one term equal to the digit times its place value, listed from the most significant place downward.
hints: Think about what each digit position contributes on its own.
A nonzero digit at a given place becomes one component: that digit scaled by the corresponding power of ten.
Walk the digits from most to least significant, and for each nonzero digit emit digit times its place value into the output list.

edge_inputs.description: trailing zeros still carry place value

stress.description: random large integers across the full range
